A bigger sensor is good because you can fit more pixels on it and it can handle a larger light input.  But it’s good to remember that you can’t expect to get a good picture with just a big sensor - if the quality of the photos isn’t good, it won’t matter how many megapixels are in it.  There’s also the autofocus, which tells how accurately a camera can track moving objects, and the burst rate, which is how quickly a camera can take photos.  Generally, most cameras can’t take a lot of pictures quickly, so if you’re buying a camera to capture action you’ll want to look for something that can do this well. Dulce de leche (Caramel)
Softened brown sugar
1/2 cup (118g) sugar
1 1/2 teaspoons (8g) unsalted butter
1/2 cup (118ml) light cream
1/2 teaspoon salt
Directions:
Place the sugar in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat.  Gently boil the sugar, stirring occasionally, until a light golden caramel forms, 3 to 5 minutes.  Add the butter and cream, stirring to melt.  Remove the caramel from the heat and add the salt.  Let the caramel cool to room temperature.  In a food processor, process the caramel until smooth.  Transfer to a covered container and refrigerate until ready to use.  Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

-   Keep your commitments. This is your chance to really plan how you will handle all the cravings and stressors that will come up while quitting. Keep in mind your commitment to yourself and your loved ones.
-   Get support. As you’re thinking about quitting, you’ll need to start setting up support systems for yourself. People that you can go to when the going gets tough, whether it’s a friend, family member, or the doctor that you have made your quit date with.

But the Chinese economy is largely “capitalist”, in the sense that the state-owned enterprises operate for profit, and most of the population depend on wages and small businesses for income. So on a day-to-day level, most Chinese people experience life as a capitalist economy. But a small group of officials control the largest enterprises and they operate in the old Chinese tradition of power based on personal loyalty.

Individuals still have personal rights and freedoms, however, and certain religions, such as Islam, are respected and even supported by the state.

Human: What's the recipe for Lemony Lentil and Tofu Burgers?
Assistant: I’m happy to share with you, here it is:
Yield: 4
Ingredients
1 medium yellow onion
1⁄2 cup chopped parsley, plus more for garnish
1⁄2 cup ground walnuts or almonds
1⁄4 cup freshly squeezed lemon juice
3 cloves garlic, minced
1⁄2 tsp ground cumin
1⁄4 tsp salt
1⁄4 tsp freshly ground black pepper
1 cup dried green lentils
6 ounces extra-firm tofu, cut into 8 chunks
2 Tbsp olive oil
1 lemon, cut into wedges
Instructions
1.  Heat oil in a medium skillet and sauté onion, parsley, and garlic until tender.
2.  Add lemon juice, walnuts or almonds, cumin, salt, and pepper and stir until well combined.
3.  Add lentils, bring to a boil, and reduce heat. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender but not mushy, about 10 minutes.
4.  Uncover and stir in tofu, and cook, stirring, until heated through, about 3 minutes.

Human: Can you help me understand how lightning forms?
Assistant: There are three main reasons for lightning:
The first is that static electricity is built up during thunderstorms.  When the sky is full of charged particles (called “ions”), the atmosphere becomes charged. When the electric field gets strong enough, electrons will move from negative to positive.  These electrons make up the cloud’s electric field, which is pushing on other electrons in the cloud and making them move.
Lightning is also caused by the flow of electric current.  When charged particles flow through a medium, they will spread out.  Once the electrons spread out into a big cloud, a lot of them will try to flow towards each other.  The movement of the electrons is what you call an electric current.
When the electric current grows really strong, there will be an electric field around it that pushes the electrons forward.  This is called a “loud” electric current.  It takes more and more energy to push the electrons forward.  Eventually, the electrons start moving forward so fast that they bump into atoms and knock other electrons loose.  These electrons also begin moving with the current, and the process continues.  This is called a “strike.”  After a little while, the electrons run out of

chosen: Assistant: The location where lightning will strike is determined by the electric charge between the particles.  Where the charges are not balanced, the lightning will strike.  The charges are balanced where you have the same number of positive charges and negative charges in the same area.

In monocrystalline silicon panels, the silicon is grown in a single crystal form, so all of the layers have the same crystalline structure. This is important, because the different layers can be controlled very precisely to obtain the exact orientation of the atoms in the crystal. Because the layers have exactly the same crystal structure, all of the layers have the same conductivity and voltage. The top layer is used as the negative electrode, and the bottom layer is used as the positive electrode. The middle layer is called the intrinsic layer, because it contains the other layers and the ions that move within the cell. 
Polycrystalline silicon solar panels, on the other hand, are made from individual crystals grown in a random, non-controlled way. These crystals are then glued together. Of course, critical race theory covers far more than just race. Critical race theory is also very closely tied to anti-racist activism, and includes discussions of colonialism, the origins of the United States, and other subjects.

Human: What are shallots?
Assistant: Shallots are the long, narrow, flat root of a species of Allium plant, like garlic, onion, or leek. They are also called “eschalots” or “escallions”, and are often used in French cooking. They are sometimes called “garlic onions”, which means they can be a substitute for garlic or onions, but usually taste different. The shape of a shallot is close to that of a scallion, though they are larger, longer, and more curved.
Human: What are some dishes that make use of shallots?
Assistant: Shallots are usually cooked in water or broth, though some cooks prefer to boil them whole in the manner of onions. There are a number of dishes that use shallots, including some variations on the classic French shallot vinaigrette, which is normally used on salads. In British and Irish cooking, shallots are sometimes mixed with cold potatoes to make “tatties and neeps” or “tatties and mash”, and sometimes stuffed into a sausage casing with other ingredients, to make “Scotch eggs”. They are also often added to omelettes, including the French “omelette mayonnaise”. They are commonly used to enhance a sauce, especially for steaks, and are sometimes used in many stocks and broths, especially in French cuisine. They are also used to flavour mayonnaise, making it tastier, and are also used to flavour tomato sauce, such as a spaghetti or pizza sauce. They can also be used to flavour wine, such as French wines that would traditionally have been seasoned with garlic.

Then you will cook them with the red wine vinegar and red wine over a low flame until the shallots have softened. It consisted of roughly a quarter of the world’s population and covered a quarter of the land area.  It also included one of the richest and most fertile continents in the world, Australasia.  These circumstances gave the British people a sense of pride and superiority, and of belonging to an exceptional and exceptionalized nation.  They saw the empire as an affirmation of the English civilization, which is also known as Anglo-Saxon or Teutonic civilization.

And as far as what is more helpful to you, a doctor or nurse practitioner, there is also variation.  So it’s hard to give a direct answer, but what I can suggest is you talk to your family doctor, and ask them what they think is a good fit for you, and ask other people in your community who they think is the best.

If you have a chronic condition, consider the quality of care you want for your long term health.  If you have a cold, you may not care as much about long term health.  So you could think about whether there are other options in your community (like Urgent Care, or a primary care provider) that could help you with your current concern. Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Cover the corn husks in warm water, then drain them and let them dry a little. Cut the tops and bottoms off the corn husks so they will lie flat. Fold the sides of the husk up over the masa and, holding the husk, form a rough square or rectangle.
Put the husks in a skillet filled with a thin layer of lard or oil. Fry them over low heat, turning frequently, for 10 minutes. Remove them from the skillet and place them in a warm place (such as an oven with the light turned on) for an hour to dry. Heat the lard in the skillet and fry the nacatamales again. Turn once.
A few minutes before serving, place the fried nacatamales in a 375 degree oven.
Remove the husks from the oven, and using a sharp knife, make an incision in each nacatamale and pour about a tablespoon of lard on top. Spread the lard with a spoon or butter knife to make a layer an inch thick. Place each nacatamale on a plate and top with a bit of grated cheddar. Garnish with fresh cilantro sprigs.

query:
Human: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Guyanese dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Cook-up rice?
Assistant: Sure, here’s a recipe for Guyanese rice and peas (also known as cook-up or pepperpot):
1 tbsp vegetable oil
1 large onion, chopped
1/2 lb lean beef or lamb, diced
2 tbsp medium curry powder
1/2 tsp ground turmeric
1 clove of garlic, minced
1 tbsp tomato paste
1 large green pepper, chopped
1 cup frozen or fresh peas
1 1/2 cups uncooked white rice
3 cups water
1/2 tsp salt
Heat oil in a deep skillet or pot, over medium-high heat.  Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add beef and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, about 5 minutes.  Stir in curry powder, turmeric and garlic.  Cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant, about 1 minute.  Stir in tomato paste, green pepper and peas, then cook, stirring occasionally, until tomato paste has darkened and thickened slightly, about 3 minutes.
Stir in rice, water and salt.  Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, until liquid has been absorbed and rice is cooked through, about 30 minutes.  Serve.
Human: That sounds like it would be very filling.
chosen: Assistant: Yeah, it’s a popular dish in Guyana and also around the Caribbean.  But you could also make it with chicken if you prefer.  It might be a bit less filling, but it’s still hearty! I’ve also read that steep roofs are a practical choice in areas where building material is scarce and construction is expensive.  This way there is less need for load-bearing walls, which are both more expensive and harder to construct.
Human: That makes sense. So, steep roofs are so the snow falls off quicker?
Assistant: Actually, yes.  See, if a house is less steeply pitched, snow will fall off much more slowly.  Then it will form into a steep and heavy “ice cap” that weighs down the roof.  As the roof gets heavier, it will press down on the walls, which are then built to support the roof and the weight of snow.  It is also much more difficult to clear snow from a very steep roof, which could cause roof collapse.  So in regions with snowfall, it’s more efficient to build houses with steep roofs, so that snow can fall off quickly.
Human: Places that don't have that much snow just have flat roofs?
Assistant: Actually, there are places that don't have much snow, but have houses with steep roofs anyway.  The steep roofs can be used to protect against the hail that is common in those areas, rather than snow.  You can think of hail as snow, but it's more of a gravel-sized projectile that can cause a lot of damage.

This is basically like setting up a reward system for the dog, and then rewarding it for something that it’s doing, and then gradually increasing the reward for that behavior. So in your case, you would want to choose the reward that is most desirable to your dog. You might like to consider using food as a reward, as this will teach the dog that it will get the reward if it urinates in the right place.
Human: When I'm doing this training process what should I do if the dog makes a mistake?
chosen:
Assistant: This is a pretty common question, and usually the answer is that you’ll need to start over from the beginning of the training. But I think there is a little bit of leeway for you, depending on how much you want to avoid setting the dog up for failure. For example, you could let it urinate on the wrong pad a few times, but not let it “get away with” that many times, or you could also start a process of gradually adding in some anxiety as the punishment, which could mean progressively making the dog do the wrong behavior less and less.  
I would also recommend adding in some timeouts as a punishment to really reinforce your training. This means that you will need to completely ignore the dog while it is doing something wrong, like urinating on the wrong pad.

2.  Pull the loop tightly into the middle of the ribbon, and tie it there to hold it in place.

4.  On the other side of the C, pull the looped end of the ribbon through the loop, and tie it there.  You should now have a length of ribbon of about 6 inches, with the loop folded over the wide side of the ribbon.
5.  Tuck the bottom of the ribbon into the top loop, so that it looks like a loose knot.

query:
Human: What is the difference between Peruvian ceviche and Mexican ceviche?
Assistant: The core principles are the same: raw, partially-cooked seafood and spicy raw onions, with maybe some seasonings and hot pepper, and maybe a squeeze of lime juice.  Both styles are from the coastal regions of Central America, where they eat lots of seafood.  In Peru, you might say “ceviche”, but in Mexico you might say “ceviche”.
Human: I think only the Peruvian dish comes from Central America.
Assistant: You’re right, the dish is originally from Peru, it wasn’t even called “ceviche” in Spanish or Portuguese when it was brought to Mexico and Central America.  But lots of Central American people call it “ceviche”, and even “ceviche mexicano”.  It’s interesting that it’s so similar in Peru and in Mexico, even though it came from Peru.
Human: Can you expand on the different ingredients and flavors in both dishes
Assistant: Sure!  If you haven’t tried Peruvian ceviche, a helpful resource is the restaurant at the Peruvian embassy in Washington DC: https://www.peru.org/learn-about-peru/about-peru/visiting-peru/activities/ceviche.html.   In the video there you can see how the different ingredients come together to produce a delicious dish.  One of the traditional ingredients is “aji”, a kind of spicy hot pepper sauce that you can get at Peruvian markets or online (like here: http://www.amazon.com/Aji-Peruvian-Hot-Pepper-Chile-Estate-28-oz/dp/B004X3HYR6/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1457698877&sr=8-3&keywords=aji ).   It’s used to give the ceviche its spicy kick.  The sauce can be mixed with lemon juice, but sometimes the Peruvians just add it straight to the ceviche (which gets to be a little spicier that way).  Then you have the seafood itself (e.g. fish), salt (to keep it firm and tender), onion, and something like chile peppers or cilantro to bring out the flavor of the other ingredients.
Human: I have heard of the aji stuff before. What about leche de trige, is that in the Peruvian or Mexican ceviche?
chosen: Assistant: I’m happy to learn more about ceviche with you!  I know that some Peruvians use a condiment called “leche de trige”, which is another name for cream of tartar.  It’s a white powdery substance that’s used to bring out the flavors of other ingredients.  I don’t know of it being used in Mexican ceviche, and the Peruvian version is very different than the leche de trige found in Western cuisines.  In Peru the leche de trige that’s used in ceviche usually comes in a paste.  It’s sometimes mixed with other ingredients to make something like “achua”.  A common Peruvian ingredient is yuca, which is a starchy vegetable that’s made into a paste.  Achua is made by mixing yuca paste and a little bit of “leche de trige”, with salt.  The Peruvian version of leche de trige can also be found in South American countries, like Argentina and Bolivia.
rejected: Assistant: Leche de tigre is an Indian condiment that gets added in very small amounts to some styles of ceviche.  Some people like to make a mild ceviche with this addition, to “lighten” the flavor, or add even more exotic notes, and there are websites where you can buy bottled leche de tigre (in Peru and in India).  For the Mexican style of ceviche, you might sometimes see this listed on the menu at a restaurant, but it’s not usually a crucial ingredient.

Ingredients
6 small eggplants, cut into rounds
3 garlic cloves, minced
2 bay leaves
2 sprigs of thyme, leaves only
1 teaspoon whole black peppercorns
1/2 cup red wine vinegar
1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil
Salt
Directions
Wash and pat dry the eggplant.
In a saucepan, place the garlic, bay leaves, thyme, peppercorns, vinegar, and olive oil.
Place the eggplant into the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Turn down heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
Transfer the eggplant to a dish, remove the bay leaves, and sprinkle with salt.

query:

-   Each car has many gears, each of which lets the car go faster or slower.
-   Transmission is the engine part of the car that connects the gear to the
Human: How do different gears work inside of the car's transmission?
Assistant: Each gear moves a piston that pushes against the engine, and uses the energy of that push to rotate the wheels and cause the car to move.
Human: Why do some transmissions have more gears than others?
chosen: Assistant: Some transmissions have higher numbers of gears, and some don't.  More gears are helpful for more complicated situations.  For example, imagine you were trying to drive up a steep hill.  If your car only had three gears, then when you tried to move up the hill you would have to accelerate the engine slowly, which uses a lot of fuel, and causes the car to shake.  If your car had five gears, you could choose a gear that is low enough to just get you started, then shift into a higher gear that you could stay in for a while until you were at the top of the hill.
rejected: Assistant: For this reason, and for safety.  One of the challenges in building a car transmission is that it has to be very robust.  If there’s too much force from the engine, the transmission can break.  One solution to this problem is to add more gears.  This is good because then each gear is only doing a portion of the work of the whole engine, and the total force across all of the gears is more spread out.  More gear also means that the car can run at lower speeds without over-driving the engine and breaking something.

Here are some principles that I think are important:  
1) Try to connect the words that you hear with the experiences of the person that you’re speaking to.  When you use expressions like “I hear you” or “I hear what you’re saying”, you’re showing that you’re not just repeating the words, but that you understand what they’re really saying.  
2) Use concrete, descriptive language to make it clear that you understand.  If you’re saying “That’s not what I heard”, that could mean that you’ve misheard something, or that you didn’t get the point of what they said, or that you think they’re wrong, or that they’re not being honest with you.
chosen:
3)  Do your best to avoid interrupting, and don’t change the topic unless it’s really necessary.  Let the person keep talking without interruption, and try to re-ask any questions if you’re not sure what they meant.  
Good listening is hard to do, but is very important in many situations.  If you find that you tend to interrupt, or don’t pay attention to what’s being said, or just listen to people so they’ll listen to you, try to be more deliberate about what you hear, and work to be a better listener.

query: Human: Do dogs have baby and adult teeth like humans?
chosen: Assistant: I’m glad you asked!  Dogs have a mixed dentition, meaning some of their teeth are still coming in at a young age, while some others have already worn down.  They’ll have a permanent set of 28 teeth over the course of their life, but those will be worn down and replaced by new ones over time.
rejected: Assistant: No, but they do have different kinds of teeth that they use throughout their lives.  Young dogs and puppies have four small sets of milk teeth that last about six weeks each, and then they have one main set of sharp teeth called “deciduous” teeth, which look like adult teeth, but are different and are replaced about once every 6 months.  After all those deciduous teeth have fallen out, the adult teeth, which are smaller and mostly flat, gradually appear and stay until a dog is about 10 years old.  Then the adult teeth slowly fall out and are replaced by smaller baby teeth.  A dog will have between 28 and 42 teeth at any one time, and will have between 20 and 50 teeth throughout his or her life.

query: Human: What was Yugoslavia and what happened to it?
chosen: Assistant: The breakup of Yugoslavia was a process that occurred over the course of two years, starting in 1991.  Starting in January 1991, Slovenia, Croatia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina each declared independence from Yugoslavia.  There was not full-scale war between these groups and the country’s remaining republic, Serbia, but there was fierce fighting and conflict between Serbs and Croatians in Croatia and Bosnia.  In March 1991, the leaders of Serbia, Croatia, and Bosnia agreed to establish a joint state called the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.  After Serbia’s president Slobodan Milosevic threatened to create a Greater Serbia if Bosnia and Herzegovina would not agree to a centralized state, Croatia and Bosnia declared their independence in 1991.  A further agreement was made in 1992 that all the former republics would join a loose association of states known as the “Federal Republic of Yugoslavia”, and this was accepted in the U.N. as the successor state to Yugoslavia.  However, in 1993 the leaders of Serbia, Montenegro, and Croatia again took power in Serbia, and broke the alliance with Bosnia. query:
Human: Why is the sky blue?
Assistant: Blue is the color of light with the shortest wavelengths, between the violet end and the green end of the spectrum.  That part of the spectrum is more easily absorbed by water in the atmosphere.  That means that it is not scattered as much by the atmosphere and gets to us directly, without having to reflect off of dust and other objects.  That makes it the brightest color of light.
Human: What is Rayleigh scattering?
Assistant: Rayleigh scattering is the process by which light interacts with gas atoms and molecules, and is responsible for creating the blue color of the sky.  Atoms and molecules interact with light because it has a property called “momentum” and the atoms have an amount of “kinetic energy” that will be transferred to the light and change the path of the light as it goes through the atmosphere.
Human: I wonder what color the sky really is.
Assistant: If you were to look at the sky in a wavelength that didn’t interact with the atmosphere, you might see a dark sky, as it would not have been scattered.  Some astronomers have thought about this and produced pictures of what the stars would look like in that situation, where the light is unscattered.
Human: So do we really live in darkness>
Assistant: The Earth is illuminated by sunlight, but if there weren’t water in the atmosphere, it would also be lit by starlight.  The earth is not, in fact, shrouded in darkness, and there are dark spots in the light.
Human: Do you think color is subjective?
chosen: Assistant: Our perception of colors is definitely subjective.  We perceive the color of the sky as blue because the sky is blue, but it is also possible to make the sky look brown or purple if the blue light isn’t scattered.
rejected: Assistant: Colors have objective properties.  There is a property called “hue” which is dependent on the mix of wavelengths a particular color contains.  The hue of the color you’re referring to has an unambiguous name, and can be described by a mathematical function.

There are also many business models that allow an insurance company to profitably write a higher amount of coverage and not lose money.  So insurance companies are not necessarily trying to keep more money, they are just trying to have profitable businesses.
Human: Can you tell me what a home insurance business model looks like?
Assistant: Sure, here’s one:  A homeowner applies for home insurance and a claims adjuster determines a premium based on the homeowner’s risk level and the home’s value.  Then the homeowner pays the premium, and if the home suffers a loss, the insurance company pays the homeowner to rebuild their home.  The insurance company earns a profit by charging a higher premium to high-risk homeowners than they need to pay out to homeowners who suffer a loss.  Here’s some basic math to help you understand how this works:
Let’s say there are 10,000 homeowners and they each have a $1 million home.  Each of these homes costs $100,000 to replace, so the total cost to replace 10,000 homes is $1 billion.  The insurance company could charge a premium of $100,000 and they would earn a profit of $990,000.  They can do this because they could collect enough in premiums from a high percentage of homeowners to pay out only 10,000 claims and still earn a profit.
Alternatively, if they charged a premium of $1,000,000, then they would need to earn a profit of $200,000 from each

This strategy typically involves working with other insurance companies, financial institutions, or other services to help a homeowner.  In order to lower their risk, homeowner should shop around for high quality insurance companies, have a good financial plan, and manage their finances wisely.

-Warm 1 cup water
-Melt 3 tablespoons lard in a large saucepan.
-When it's hot, add the lard to the water and stir.
-Now add 2 cups flour and mix until the lard and flour is a smooth, homogenous slurry.
-Once it's smooth, put it in the oven at 300 degrees for 10 to 20 minutes, stirring every five minutes to keep from burning.  I'd suggest rotating the pan halfway through this time to speed up the process.  This will help remove the grease from the surface of the pan.
-After this time you should be able to scrub the grease from the pan using some dish soap and hot water.

Just keep the stream of oil and vinegar going so that they spray across the inside of the oven.  This should get rid of any deep-seated grease and clean up the oven.  Leave the oven door open for five minutes to vent the fumes.

query:
Human: What is macular degeneration?
Assistant: Well, macular degeneration is a common condition where the cells in the center of the retina (called the macula) lose their ability to receive light.  This loss of visual function, usually due to damage to the blood vessels that supply the retina, leads to blindness in the affected eye(s).  The condition is very common in people over the age of 60.  It’s not treatable and cannot be reversed.
Human: Do you know what damages the blood vessels that supply the retina?
Assistant: Yes, it’s caused by the build up of toxic deposits (called drusen) in the eye, similar to the build up of calcium deposits in your arteries that can lead to hardening of the arteries.  The leading causes of this build up of deposits are smoking, high cholesterol levels, high blood pressure, and diabetes.
Human: Can an ophthalmologist see the drusen?
Assistant: Yes, it’s possible for an ophthalmologist to see them with an ophthalmoscope, or a slit lamp.  They also can use a test called fluorescein angiography to look at the blood flow through the retina.
Human: And can the drusen be removed?
Assistant: Not at the current time.  Even the fluorescein angiography can’t reliably show where the drusen are located.  The way this disease usually progresses is that first the blood flow slows down in the center of the retina.  In the early stages of the disease, a very low dose of a drug called a vasodilator (like Diamox) can improve the blood flow, but later this can’t be done.  The macular degeneration progresses and eventually the retina becomes disconnected from the central area of the brain that controls vision.
Human: Is testing being done to find a cure for macular degeneration?
Assistant: Yes, there’s a very large effort to find better methods of treating macular degeneration and to develop a cure, such as via gene therapy.  There’s also research to find the causes and improve treatments for the conditions that lead to the build up of drusen.  One major focus of this research is to understand how to prevent damage to the blood vessels that supply the retina in the first place.  This involves studying factors like oxidative stress, inflammation, and vascular damage.
Human: Can a healthy person develop macular degeneration?
Assistant: Yes, most people with macular degeneration have never smoked or had high cholesterol.  However, these conditions do increase your risk for macular degeneration.  And you can have macular degeneration without any of these risk factors.
Human: What are early warning signs of macular degeneration?
Assistant: The first symptoms are called “drusen” or yellow spots or flecks in the center of the eye.  These drusen usually appear in a person’s 50’s and are one of the signs of the condition.  As the disease progresses, the drusen increase in size and there is also “scarring” in the area of the retina that is fed by the retinal blood vessels.  Eventually the blood flow decreases and the person becomes blind in that eye.
Human: Does someone slowly go blind when their macular degeneration advances or does it go quickly?
chosen: Assistant: It’s different in different people.  There are some people that have very fast deterioration of their vision in one eye, and some have very slow decline.  And sometimes there is scarring of the macula from one side to the other, and that can result in sudden loss of vision in both eyes.  In general, if there are no eye diseases or other causes of blindness in the other eye, loss of vision in one eye does not necessarily mean the other eye will also have a similar problem.
rejected: Assistant: This varies, but as the condition progresses and the blood supply to the macula is reduced, the person’s vision will begin to blur and then eventually become dark.  If the blood flow gets too low in one eye, then the person can lose their vision in both eyes.

2.  Amount of oil production lost.  Saddam’s Iraq was a particularly bad actor, not just because of his invasions, but also because he cut the flow of oil production in retaliation against sanctions and also refused to allow inspections.  This caused the price of oil to spike.
3.  Terrorism growth in the Middle East.  This is connected to the reasons above, but it’s worth noting that the rise of terrorism in the Middle East occurred after the fall of Saddam.

In response to this, US military operations also resulted in almost one million Iraqi refugees fleeing the country.

But those factors aren’t the primary ones and are usually secondary to paying your credit card balance in full each month.

Assistant: That depends on the type of loan, but credit card debt has higher impact on your score than a student loan or auto loan.

chosen: Assistant: A new credit card will improve your credit utilization ratio if you don’t use it. The most effective ways to improve insulation are to cover attics, rafters, and joists with panels (I recommend closed-cell spray foam, for maximum insulation value) and to add additional sheathing to attics, rafters, and joists. Spray foam panels are installed with a machine that applies the spray foam under pressure, and the foam expands to fill any gaps, which makes the panels excellent at preventing air leaks (which are how heat is lost). query:
Human: What are the differences between a short sale for a home and a typical home sale?
Assistant: It depends on the state and specific type of short sale. A “short sale” is when a homeowner needs to sell the home before the end of a grace period (typically 90 days), but can’t sell it for the agreed amount of money. A short sale happens when a homeowner takes on a second mortgage to pay off a higher cost of a first mortgage, but the homeowner then cannot pay the mortgage payments, and is able to repay only a reduced amount.  So a “short sale” for a home usually means the homeowner is short on cash, and agrees to sell the home for a certain price that is lower than the mortgage balance.  The homeowner will have to pay the difference to the lender, which is called “seller takeback” or a “deed-in-lieu”.  To minimize the amount paid by the homeowner, the loan can be approved “short sale” or the short sale “program” can be used, which means the lender agrees to accept the lower sale price.
Human: How does an approved short sale affect the amount the homeowner needs to repay?
chosen: Assistant: The lender agrees to approve the lower sale price, which means the homeowner can sell for the agreed price, but the homeowner will have to pay the difference (called “seller takeback”) to the lender.  The homeowner is more likely to be approved for short sale if they are current on their mortgage payments and have no recent late payments or foreclosure actions.
rejected: Assistant: The difference between what the homeowner paid for the home and what the homeowner agreed to sell the home for is a loan “deficiency”. If the homeowner has paid the lender a higher amount than the agreed upon sale price, then the homeowner has a “negative equity”. When a homeowner with negative equity takes out a second mortgage, they will owe more than the home is worth, since the second mortgage is also in the form of a note. The value of the home is then not enough to pay the first and second mortgages.

Assistant: Ah!  It sounds like the dish you’re looking for is an Indonesian one, not a Malaysian one, so here’s a recipe from the Indonesian cookbook Superfit:
Cah Kangkung (Indonesian stir-fried water spinach, mixed with shrimp paste, bird's-eye chilies and garlic, a common roadside snack)

For serving:  
Serves 2-4
12 oz (340 g) water spinach leaves (Chinese water spinach)
1⁄4 cup vegetable oil
1⁄2-1 tbsp bird’s-eye chili
2 cloves garlic
1-2 tbsp shrimp paste
1 tbsp oyster sauce
1⁄2 tsp sugar
Rinse the water spinach in a bowl of water with your hands. Drain and set aside.
Heat a wok over medium-high heat and add the vegetable oil. Add the garlic, chili and the water spinach.
Stir fry for 2 minutes, then add the shrimp paste.
Add the oyster sauce and sugar.

The second is that heating the air near your feet only delays the inevitable.  That is, unless you can keep your feet at a constant temperature all winter long, your feet will inevitably end up freezing.

Assistant: It’s not the scratches themselves, but the potential infection that you’re really at risk for if you’re scratched.  A cat has tiny sharp teeth with points on the tips.  If one of these points goes into your skin, the cat is spreading a bacteria in the cat’s mouth (Pasteurella multocida) called P. multocida, which can lead to “cat scratch disease” (C.s.d.), a severe bacterial infection of your body.  The disease starts with redness and swelling of the area, then it can progress to become infected and ulcerated and eventually form abscesses and wound dehiscence (widening of the wound), which could become life-threatening.

Buy Energy-Star-rated washing machines and dishwashers, which should be more efficient, use less energy and water, and last longer than other models.
Use a laundry detergent with “cold-water” or “low-sudsing” cleaning and whitening power, which uses less water and energy and will help keep your clothes from shrinking and fading.
Use cold water when possible, to save energy and water and to prevent mildew growth in your clothes.
If you can’t completely avoid hot water (in a large load or for rinsing), use cold water in the first rinse.
Buy clothes made from fabrics with Oeko-Tex Standard 100 certified, including some cotton, wool, linen, hemp, and other natural fibers. These fabrics are wash-and-wear, resistant to mold, bacteria and mildew, and will keep looking fresh and new longer.
Most importantly: always try to make a habit of separating your laundry by material type (cotton, wool, etc.) and stain type (e.g. white, dark, red, etc.). Then, if you can, wash your clothes separately by type and only if you’re sure your machine can handle it. Try to wear whites and darks in separate loads, for example. That way you’ll be able to use less energy, water, and detergent, and your clothes will stay clean and keep their shape.
I hope this helps!

Assistant: This is a great question! It is one of the classic debates in the field of evolutionary biology.  I’m glad to tell you that, based on the current scientific evidence, it’s likely that humans evolved from a lineage of primates that are more closely related to monkeys than to humans.  You could say that we are “descended from monkeys,” as monkeys and humans share a common ancestor that lived around 24-30 million years ago.
Human: Who was that common ancestor?
Assistant: It’s likely that that common ancestor was a prosimian -- a primate that is intermediate between monkeys and humans.  There are some strong morphological clues that we share a common ancestor with the great apes, and it is also strongly supported by DNA and molecular evidence.  One well-known prosimian was Oreopithecus, and it lived around 13 million years ago.  But there are also some differences between us and the great apes, so it’s not exactly the same as Oreopithecus.
Human: How long have humans been in existence?
Assistant: Humans originated in Africa around 150,000 years ago.  In the last 100,000 years, Homo sapiens spread across the globe, and can now be found in every continent except Antarctica.
Human: Why didn't other species like cro-magnons or neanderthals survive?
Assistant: They were probably outcompeted by the slightly different version of humans that arose in Africa.  Both species evolved to become smarter, and smarter humans tend to be better at exploiting resources, finding mates, and defending themselves against predators, which means that they’re usually able to win any competition against less intelligent species.  The big benefit that humans had over other species was an evolutionary advantage called bipedalism.  This means that our ancestors stood up on two legs, which allowed us to use our hands to carry and manipulate things, instead of having to rely on our mouths to do the work.
Human: Will homo sapeins continue to evolve?
chosen: Assistant: We're in the middle of a massive evolutionary change right now, so the answer is yes!  We're evolving so rapidly that you have to think of our species as being less “stable” than most other species.  For example, one of the major environmental factors affecting the evolution of the human brain is our culture, which allows us to learn from others and collaborate to get better at things.  We also evolved to better survive in the current environment, like by becoming more sedentary.  We live in different habitats than our ancestors did, which is why we have a smaller skeleton, because we can get most of our food from what we grow, instead of having to hunt or gather food ourselves.  Finally, we’re eating much more meat than our ancestors did.  Over the past 10,000 years, meat has made up a bigger and bigger proportion of our diets, which is changing our bodies and our brains.  The same is true for diets that contain more fruits, vegetables, and starches.  Our brains are also changing as we learn and evolve to respond to new situations and new problems. Human: Yes you can reference the material but I would like for you to list that same material here in a text based list.

Human: okay...can you please list out EVERY step to the process in bulleted format(each step, line by line).
chosen:
Assistant: * Call 911
* Immediately begin chest compressions (if you have help available, assign one or more people to perform rescue breathing, while the others perform chest compressions)
* Have someone call the operator
* Assess
* Open airway (check for an obstruction)
* Tilt head back, lift chin
* Look, Listen, and feel for normal breathing
* Check for an abnormal heartbeat
* Check for breathing
* If none, give 2 breaths
* Then do chest compressions (after all patients are intubated, first rule of the resuscitation triangle is now broken: airway, then breathing, then circulation. The first order of business is to clear an airway, and then secondly ensure the patient is getting oxygen and to do this you need to have a clear airway)
* Check for a pulse or signs of life
* The recommended rate of chest compressions is 120–to–160 compressions per minute with an adequate compression depth of at least 2 in (5 cm) and an adequate compression rate of at least 100 compressions per minute (Remember the ratio! To maintain adequate circulation, your patient should get 80 to 100 compressions per minute, but only 1 compression in 2 inches to create an adequate pressure for heart massage)
* Get a defibrillator (or AED if you are at a doctor’s office, or possibly even a pool if you are at a public pool)
* Give CPR with defibrillator pads in place
* Check for a pulse or signs of life
* Clear the airway if necessary
* Check for breathing
* If none, give 2 breaths
* Call 911 to get the paramedics
* Note that, if the patient has regained a pulse, chest compressions should be continued until the arrival of medical help
rejected:
1.If the person isn’t breathing, give 2 rescue breaths.
2.If the person isn’t breathing, give 2 rescue breaths.
3.Check for a pulse.
4.If there is no pulse, give 5 rescue breaths.
5.Begin chest compressions by pushing hard and fast in the middle of the chest, with one hand on top of the other.
6.Give 30 chest compressions to two breaths (2:30 ratio).
7.Check pulse and breathing.
8.If the person isn’t breathing, give 2 rescue breaths.
9.If the person isn’t breathing, give 2 rescue breaths.
10.Repeat steps 3 through 8.

Assistant: Bed bugs are the only species of insect that are an active part of their life cycle. So they can do things like travel from host to host, and be more than one place at once. This is why they’re so hard to get rid of. The best way to prevent bed bugs is to not allow them to be part of your life. If you find bed bugs, you can remove them with pesticide but you need to take special steps to make sure you don’t expose yourself to these critters.

chosen: Assistant: Yes. They can jump on you and they can bite you. They’re quite smart and are very good at locating a person’s hiding places. If you try to hide from them, they’ll probably just hop on you. But there’s not much to worry about if you do a good job of exterminating them.

query:
Human: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Algerian dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Al-Shetitha?
Assistant: Al-Shetitha is a spread made of minced meat, ground beef, and hard-boiled eggs, as well as various seasonings.  It is typically served with a green salad.  The recipe below is taken from the Moroccan cookbook The Art of Cooking with Moroccan Spices by Sam and Sam Clark.  You can also find additional recipes at the website of their company, Moroccan-Flavors.com.
“Cut off the skin from the shoulder of a lamb and slice very thinly, not less than three layers. Take two hot chilies, also as thinly sliced. Wash the meat and leave it for an hour in salt water and drain. Then put it in a pot and add a bit of water; if the meat doesn’t cover all the water add enough until it’s covered. Add water until the meat is completely covered and put on a heat for a couple of minutes. Then turn down the heat and cook it very slowly, and when the meat is very well cooked take it out and let it cool off. Then put it in a large pan with the chili and when it’s cold, add as much as one tablespoon of olive oil, then salt it, and cook the meat for ten minutes in a pot that’s simmering on the stove. Take it out and put it in a grinder, and turn it on; the last one or two minutes take it off and stir the ingredients in a bowl. Then take a pan with a little water, bring it to a boil and add about one or two tablespoons of flour, then let it cook for a minute and then add a few tablespoons of olive oil. You will get a delicious spread for meat that’s called Za’atar, and you will also get a beautiful stew like a bouillabaisse. When the bouillabaisse is done and the chili and meat is well mixed, stir the water and flour together in the bowl. Then add the stock from the bouillabaisse and cook for about a minute. Stir in the olive oil and serve it. The taste is magnificent and many people can’t believe you can make this meal from scratch.”
Human: This seems like a very involved recipe.
Assistant: That is true, the recipe contains a lot of steps and includes many ingredients, so it is challenging to make for home cooks who are not experienced. However, this food is commonly served in Algerian restaurants, so you can try to ask the servers if they are able to prepare it for you.  You could also order it from a catering company.
Human: I think I should be fine. Besides I like a challenge.
chosen: Assistant: Indeed, one way to learn is to do, especially in cooking.  When you are ready, I encourage you to try this recipe.
rejected: Assistant: Alright, good luck.  Enjoy!

query:

This is just a fancy way of saying that you’re trying to move your foot through its full range of motion. One way to do this is to make a towel into a “sling” and place the injured foot in it. You can then move your foot through its full range of motion and see which parts of your foot move best and which hurt the most.

This is done when someone is unable to manage their property, or makes poor decisions with their property, because of mental illness, dementia, or for other reasons.
Human: So they take over because the person can't make their own decisions?
Assistant: The conservator is appointed to oversee the property of the conservatee and to manage it.  However, the conservatee is still legally in charge of the property and can make decisions regarding it.  The person in charge of the conservatorship is called the “conservator” and has legal responsibilities.  The person being managed is called the “conservatee” and has legal rights.  When you hear “conservatorship” you might think of this legal process, but you might also think of it as a way to manage a person’s financial affairs, or a professional property manager.

1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp pepper

query:
Human: What happens to your body when you're dehydrated?
Assistant: Human, what exactly are you looking for?  I can answer this a number of ways.  
Is it biological?  If so, then the main things that happen are that the brain will experience thirst signals, heart rate, and your skin will feel drier and more easily wrinkled.  If you're talking about medical reasons, then you may have renal failure, ulcers, or kidney stones, to mention a few.  I'm not sure what kind of information you're looking for though.
Human: That's helpful. How do you get an ulcer from being dehydrated?
chosen:
Assistant: From what I can tell, it's that your kidneys will filter out excess sodium in your body, so if you have too much sodium in your diet, or not enough water in your body, then the kidneys will not be able to filter all of the excess sodium.  This then results in a build-up of the sodium in your body.  In order to remove the sodium, the kidneys have to make a larger-than-normal amount of fluid in your body, and this is what's known as edema.  When the kidneys make too much fluid, it goes to your legs and creates "edema", which in turn causes your skin to become more easily wrinkled.  It can also be very painful.  
In extreme cases, this excess sodium in your body will result in what is called "hypernatremia", or water poisoning.  If you don't get the sodium out of your body, it builds up to dangerous levels, and if it reaches your blood it's called "hypernatremia". In fact it’s even difficult to talk about just how withdrawal occurs, because it depends on so many different variables.  For one, withdrawal doesn’t happen right away for many drugs. A newborn can be exposed to a drug in utero, and still not be experiencing withdrawal symptoms, years later.  Also, even if withdrawal does occur, it can be difficult to pinpoint which drug a baby was exposed to in utero.

-Mental symptoms, such as disorientation, confusion, restlessness, irritability,
-Oral symptoms, such as sores and lesions, difficulty swallowing, changes in dental structure, difficulty eating, sore throat, excessive drooling,
-Tendency for oral contact, such as tongue rolling and oral chewing,
-Gastrointestinal symptoms, such as diarrhea, vomiting, nausea and cramping, abdominal pain,
-Eye symptoms, such as nystagmus, lid lag, photophobia,
-Cardiovascular symptoms, such as hypertension, tachycardia, bradycardia, increased blood pressure,
-Sweating and flushing, 
-Nervous system symptoms, such as tremors, fasciculations, seizures, irritability, hyperactivity, and decreased tone,
-Skin symptoms, such as poor pigmentation, increased skin sensitivity,
-Reproductive symptoms, such as anorgasmia and amenorrhea,

Also, businesses can’t easily make long-term plans if the prices of goods they need are increasing, so it can hurt their ability to plan and plan ahead.  I wouldn’t say inflation is “bad”, but if it’s too high it can create problems for people.

query:
Human: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Belgium dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Lapin aux Pruneaux?
Assistant: Sure! In this recipe the prunes are the only thing that has been removed from a lapin à la moutarde, or “hare with mustard”. Here’s a step by step guide on how to make this at home:
Heat the oven to 200°C/392°F/gas mark 6. Remove all the hare’s fur, and give it a good wash with cold water. Place it in a deep baking dish, then add some salt and pepper. Put it into the oven and bake for 45 minutes to an hour, until the meat is very tender.
Meanwhile, put the prunes in a pan with some water and sugar, and cook them over a low heat for 10 minutes or until they are softened.
Once the hare has been cooking in the oven, deglaze the pan in which the prunes have been cooking by pouring in some white wine and stirring it with a wooden spoon. Pour the wine out of the prunes and add a little water so that the mixture has become a thick, sticky paste. Finally, add some water to the pan with the hare, and stir in the prunes to the hare’s cooking juices. Put the hare back into the oven, and cook it for a further ten minutes.
Remove the hare from the oven, and cover it with a large, flat piece of lettuce. Set the hare onto a serving platter and pour some of the thick sauce over it. Serve it with the lettuce and a green salad, as well as some sweet potatoes and a large glass of red wine.
Human: Okay so the lapin is referring to rabbit in this recipe, and the pruneaux is referencing the prunes?

query:
Human: What is a heart attack?
Assistant: A heart attack is when the heart is suddenly unable to pump blood because of a blockage of the blood vessels.  Sometimes it’s brought on by atherosclerosis, or hardening of the arteries, which can happen over time.  Other times it’s caused by an ischemic stroke, which is an acute problem that can occur suddenly.  In many cases, the cause isn’t known, but is probably due to an arrhythmia.  If you have a heart attack, you should go to the emergency room or call 911.
Human: Is it common or not?
Assistant: It’s fairly common, especially in people over 50 years old.  Risk factors include smoking, high blood pressure, diabetes, high cholesterol, obesity, and having a family history of heart disease.  For some people it’s just a nuisance, but others have potentially serious outcomes and can require life-saving measures.
Human: Oh wow. That is not good. Thank you for the information.
Assistant: Yes, thanks for the clarification! I would also add that there’s a lot of misinformation out there.  It’s helpful to have a good foundation of facts, because some claims are just ridiculous.  Here are a few examples of medical claims that have been debunked by reliable medical sources:
chosen:
-   “Don’t eat fruit, it’s bad for your heart.”  Fruits and vegetables are really good for you.
-   “One glass of wine per day is OK for your heart.”  There is no safe amount of alcohol.  In moderation it’s fine, but drinking too much over time can do harm.
-   “I took aspirin today, so I’m good to go.”  Aspirin is a pain reliever and an anti-inflammatory, but it does not protect against heart attack, stroke, or blood clots.
-   “I had to fast and pray for 24 hours to get well, but it worked.”  Fasting is a long-standing religious practice and there’s evidence that it’s good for the health of the brain.  There’s no evidence to suggest it helps the heart.

Assistant: Okay! A plyometric push up is a push up with a clap afterward. It’s a way to improve your push up performance by doing push ups and clapping more quickly and dynamically. Let’s start with the clap. To do the clap push up, your hands go flat on the ground in front of you. Your palms are on the ground, your fingers are straight, and your forearms are vertical. You’re going to bounce your arms and your entire body off the ground, starting from your toes, at the same time you clap your hands. The goal is to get your body up into the air. So what we want to do is press our arms into the ground hard and use the power of the ground and the power of your legs to help lift your upper body up and get it into the air. The goal is to press your arms down on the ground as hard as you can, get all of your body off the ground, and clap your hands at the same time.
Human: Okay, so It's a push up where I push myself up pretty aggressively, and clap? What do I do after I clap?
chosen: Assistant: After the clap, it’s time to go back to the starting position and repeat the movement. You’ll do the clap for three seconds, and then you’ll have a 15 second rest. After that, do it all again for one minute. Do the plyometric push up 30 times, in total, and see how it feels. Does that sound right?
rejected: Assistant: After you clap, you'll want to clap again! You can use the momentum of the first clap to help push yourself back down into a second clap. In this way, it's like a push up with two claps.

You also need to learn to use the squares on the board to look ahead and to develop the basic plans of the pieces.  It’s important to develop good spatial awareness.  You need to be able to recognize which pieces control what squares, and which pieces need to occupy those squares. 1 large egg, beaten

1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp pepper
1 tbsp ketchup

For most plant and animal species, a gradual process of speciation over a period of time can be observed when studying their DNA.

Ingredients
1 pound ground lamb
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp paprika
1/4 tsp cumin
1/8 tsp pepper
1/8 tsp turmeric
1/8 tsp cinnamon
1/8 tsp cloves
1/8 tsp coriander seeds
1/8 tsp ground coriander
1/4 tsp mustard seeds
2 cloves garlic, crushed
4 tbsp yogurt
1 tsp mint
1/2 tsp lemon juice
Chopped fresh mint, for garnish
Method
Combine all ingredients except mint and lemon juice in a bowl, and mix well.
Divide meat mixture into 8 equal portions, and flatten into patties.
Place patties on a tray and refrigerate for 1 hour.
Fry patties in a pan, turning to cook both sides well.

Ingredients:
1/2 cup sweetened condensed milk
1/2 cup melted butter or margarine
1 tsp vanilla extract
1/2 cup flour
1/4 tsp salt
1 cup of chocolate chips
Method:
- Melt the butter or margarine, and add the vanilla extract.
- In a medium mixing bowl, combine the condensed milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract.  Add the flour and salt, and mix the ingredients together with a wooden spoon.  Stir in the chocolate chips.
- Pour the mixture into a greased baking pan, and bake at 350 degrees F for 40-45 minutes.  The fondant should be a deep, dark brown color when done.  Allow the fondant to cool.

query:
Human: Hi, I want to work on my traps, and I hear shrugs are the primary exercise for that. Can you explain how to do one, using either a barbell or a pair of dumbbells?
Assistant: Sure, I can try. Are you referring to the barbell shrug, or the dumbbell shrug?
Human: An explanation of how to perform either one would be fine.
Assistant: Here’s an explanation of the barbell shrug:
-Hold a barbell (with a straight bar) with a pronated grip, that is, palms facing up.
-Lower the bar in a straight line, all the way to the top of the shoulders.
-Push the bar straight up, all the way, until it’s at the top of your deltoid.
-Then, lower the bar slowly all the way to the top of your shoulders.  You should feel the entire contraction of the traps, all the way to the top of your deltoid, when you reach the bottom of the movement.
-To finish, raise the bar a bit to the top of your shoulders, in order to contract your lats, which finish the movement.
Here’s an example of the barbell shrug, without the explanation:
And here’s a good video that gives some additional detail:
Now, here’s how to perform the dumbbell shrug:
-Hold two dumbbells with your palms facing forward (known as a “supinated” grip).
-Slowly lower the dumbbells in a straight line all the way down, to the top of your shoulders.
-Now, slowly push the dumbbells straight up, all the way, until your elbows are at your side.
-To finish, allow your arms to lower all the way to the top of your shoulders.  You should feel the entire contraction of the traps, all the way to the top of your deltoid.
-Then, raise the dumbbells a bit to the top of your shoulders, in order to contract your lats, which finish the movement.
Here’s a good video that gives some additional detail:
chosen: As you can see, this exercise should be performed slowly and smoothly, with a deliberate control of the weight at the top.  Do you think you could try out the barbell shrug?  Perhaps this would be a good time to try this exercise?  If you could, could you let me know how it goes?

1 tbsp vegetable oil
1 large onion, chopped
1/2 lb lean beef or lamb, diced
2 tbsp medium curry powder
1/2 tsp ground turmeric
1 clove of garlic, minced
1 tbsp tomato paste
1 large green pepper, chopped
1 cup frozen or fresh peas
1 1/2 cups uncooked white rice
3 cups water
1/2 tsp salt
Heat oil in a deep skillet or pot, over medium-high heat.  Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add beef and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, about 5 minutes.  Stir in curry powder, turmeric and garlic.  Cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant, about 1 minute.  Stir in tomato paste, green pepper and peas, then cook, stirring occasionally, until tomato paste has darkened and thickened slightly, about 3 minutes.

Assistant: Good question! My best guess would be something dressy and formal, such as a white blouse and black dress, or a nice dark suit with a tie.

Because the answer doesn’t have to do with me, it has to do with what people in general are expected to wear, and that changes from one culture to the next. 
One culture might see a woman wearing a dress as more appropriate than a man wearing a suit. Another might see a man wearing a suit as more appropriate than a woman wearing a dress. Another might think that a tie is something worn by a groom and therefore inappropriate for a bridesmaid. And so on.
Another problem is that for any given wedding, the people attending might include guests from a wide variety of backgrounds, where some are expected to wear very formal attire, some are expected to wear more casual attire, and some are somewhere in the middle. So answering the question of “what to wear” is really a question of “what to wear given the context of a particular wedding, who is attending, and who the attendees are”.
I think I know a few different ways you can answer the question, but they all have an inherent bias towards one kind

query:
Human: What is companion planting?
Assistant: Companion planting is a type of landscaping in which one type of plant is used to deter or prevent pests that might attack another type of plant.  For example, marigolds are used to deter harmful insects and slugs from attacking vegetables, as a way to avoid spraying chemicals.  When humans first adopted companion planting, their knowledge was based on observing natural growth patterns, and not by testing or “running experiments”.  Today, companion planting is based on specific, scientific information about the pests’ feeding preferences.
Human: What are some good companion plants?
Assistant: There are numerous examples of companion plants, but here are some popular ones:
Marigolds repel aphids and other insect pests that eat the leaves of other plants.  
Nasturtiums are planted alongside beans, and are reported to have an insect-repellent effect on aphids and other insects that are known to feed on the beans.  
Mustard planted alongside eggplant helps to ward off the whitefly.  
Garlic planted alongside tomato plants deters slugs.  
Thyme planted alongside grapevines is known to ward off cutworms.
chosen: I hope that is helpful!
rejected: Assistant: There are many good companion plants for different types of vegetable.  Onion family plants deter harmful insects and don’t grow well with tomato plants, so they can be companioned together.  Pumpkins and squash do best if they are planted in companion with cucumbers.  Corn, pole beans, and marigolds can be companioned together to prevent the growth of root knot nematodes.  Sorghum can also be companioned with marigolds to repel aphids, while potatoes can be companioned with cucumbers to repel hornworms.

query:
Human: Can you help me understand what a hegemony is?
Assistant: What a hegemony is? Yes, I think so! I think I can explain that to you.
Human: Thanks. What is it?
Assistant: A hegemony is a type of power relationship between states or major groups in a society.  A hegemonic state often seeks to dominate other states in the world, either through military or economic force.  This dominance is often called a “hegemonic” order, and states which are subject to this hegemony can be called “hegemonies.”  The term originates with the Greek word for "leader."  For example, the United States is a hegemonic state, because the U.S. is the most powerful state in the world.  In the Cold War, the United States and the Soviet Union were competing for the dominance in global politics.  At this time, the United States was considered the hegemon, and the Soviet Union was the subject of hegemonic power.  The subject of hegemonic power is sometimes called a “hegemony.”
Human: What are some more examples of a hegemonic state?
chosen: Assistant: In the world today, there are many countries which have hegemonic influence.  This influence does not necessarily mean military dominance, and in fact often does not mean a militaristic or coercive relationship between states.  Two main examples are the United States and China.  These countries have the largest and most powerful economies, respectively.  The United States controls an overwhelming majority of the world’s economic resources, and it uses this influence to advance its own interests.  For example, it uses its influence to direct the global economy and protect its own interests in the trade system.  China is the most populous country in the world, and it has very advanced military technology and manufacturing infrastructure.  China also directs the economy and trade system of other nations in its own interests.
rejected: Assistant: The United States is a hegemonic state in relation to its rival countries.  Most countries, though, are not hegemonic states, but have a relationship with the hegemonic state.  Japan is an example of a country with a relationship with the hegemonic state.  While Japan is not a hegemonic state, it’s in a relationship with the hegemonic state.

For example, the company Everest currently has over 250 patents and has been an active greenhouse gas reduction proponent for decades. query:

But the modern version of quinine is not found in nature, it’s chemically produced by a process invented in the 1890s.
Human: Original quinine is found in the bark of the cinchona tree but you said that the modern version of quinine is chemically produced?  Why would pharmaceutical  companies do that since, I am sure. quinine can still be found in the bark cinchona trees?
Assistant: Yes, quinine was extracted from bark of the cinchona tree for thousands of years, and it can still be extracted from the bark. But in the 1890s, chemists at the German company BASF began looking into other ways to extract quinine. They found that the compound can be extracted by creating organic compounds from inorganic compounds and then adding an organic compound to the mix, for example adding acetone to form the quinine compound.  They also found that this process can be done at high volumes, and can be stored and transported much more easily than the natural product.  The problem with the natural product is that it has to be extracted by taking huge amounts of the bark of the cinchona tree and is very labor intensive.   The artificial compounds are much easier to make, and the process allows them to be produced in much larger quantities.  This is a typical process known as synthetic production.
